title: Viewer split into editable files
question: Should the viewer stay one big generated file, or split into separate HTML/CSS/JS that read the decision data?
rationale: Promoted the refined viewer into the generator's embedded templates so new projects scaffold it by default — no top bar (floating theme/help), hero app-icon lockup, full-width search with a Sort · Filter · View cluster, and an icon view toggle. Scaffold-only-if-missing is unchanged, so existing projects keep their hand-edited files and are updated by copying the newer ones in.

options.label: Separate files that read the JSON directly (local server)

tradeoffs.note: restyle in plain CSS; edit a decision's JSON and refresh, no rebuild
the page reads each decision file; nothing copies the content
still offline, but the folder must be served locally (python3 -m http.server)
start a small local server to view; no rebuild for content edits

options.label: Separate index.html / styles.css / app.js + generated data.js

tradeoffs.note: restyle in plain CSS; no Python, no regenerating the look
data is a script include, so double-click still works with no server
data.js holds a baked copy of every decision, separate from the JSON

options.label: Keep the single generated file (leave as is)

tradeoffs.note: styling is buried in the Python; every change regenerates the whole file
